jobs.title: Remote Office Assistant
jobs.jd_full:
Coalition Technologies is seeking a reliable, detail-oriented, and highly organized Remote Office Assistant to support administrative, bookkeeping, billing, reporting, data entry, and internal operations tasks. This role is ideal for someone with strong communication skills, discretion, attention to detail, and a willingness to learn and grow within a fast-paced remote work environment.

As an Office Assistant, you will help support daily administrative operations, assist with entry-level bookkeeping, organize client documents, support internal reporting, and help maintain accurate company records. This position offers strong growth potential for the right candidate, including opportunities for advancement within the Accounting team or other departments.

We are looking for talented and diligent candidates who perform well on our skills assessments. Strong skills test performance may be considered even if a candidate does not meet every preferred experience or educational background requirement.

 

Why Join Coalition Technologies?

Coalition Technologies is committed to delivering high-quality work for our clients while providing our team with a fun, thriving, innovative, and growth-focused work environment. We offer:

  • 100% remote work with a company that has supported remote team members for more than a decade
  • Competitive profit-sharing bonus plan, with up to 50% of company profits paid to full-time employees each month
  • Competitive Paid Time Off plan that supports work-life balance
  • Subsidized gym memberships
  • Medical, dental, vision, and life insurance packages for eligible US-based team members
  • International Health Insurance Reimbursement Program for eligible international team members
  • Device upgrade and learning reimbursement programs
  • Career development plans with clearly defined goals and rewards
  • Additional job-specific incentives and bonuses

 

Office Assistant Responsibilities

As an Office Assistant, your duties will include:

  • Answering phones and emails
  • Completing entry-level bookkeeping tasks, including recording expenses, organizing receipts, and maintaining transaction records
  • Resolving billing issues with clients and internal team members
  • Providing account access, usage reports, data analysis, and other ad hoc support for team members
  • Supporting quality assurance checks for internal and client-facing reports
  • Organizing new client contracts, creating invoices, and processing client payments
  • Contributing to internal database maintenance, upkeep, and data entry
  • Researching, ordering, and distributing company-wide gifts two to three times per year
  • Organizing company events, competitions, and special projects throughout the year
  • Facilitating company holiday calendars, time-off calendars, and schedule variation calendars

 

Required Skills and Qualifications

The ideal candidate will have:

  • Willingness to learn, grow, and collaborate with the team and company as a whole
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills
  • High level of discretion, ethics, and trustworthiness
  • Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
  • Ability to support administrative, bookkeeping, billing, reporting, and data entry tasks
  • Innovative thinking and willingness to challenge existing methods when improvement is possible
  • Availability to work 40 hours per week, Monday through Friday, 9:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. Pacific Time
  • Reliable remote workspace with a fast computer, quality internet, camera, microphone, and speakers

 

Preferred Skills

  • The following experience is preferred but not required:
  • Intermediate spreadsheet skills
  • Experience with bookkeeping or financial record keeping
  • Experience with Google Sheets, Microsoft Excel, QuickBooks Online, and Google Workspace
  • Experience supporting billing, invoicing, reporting, data entry, or administrative operations

 

Remote Work Environment

Coalition Technologies is a fully remote company. Our team works remotely with the support of time tracking software, structured communication, and a company culture designed to help remote team members succeed. We have supported remote team members for more than a decade and welcome qualified applicants from around the world.

 

Growth Opportunity

This position offers a strong opportunity for growth. Successful Office Assistants at Coalition Technologies have advanced quickly and earned promotions within the Accounting team and across other departments.

 

Compensation

For candidates located in California, New York, Washington, and Colorado, the starting base pay for this position ranges from $15 to $25 per hour. Compensation may vary based on factors such as experience, qualifications, skills test performance, geographic location, and seniority of the position offered. For candidates outside of California, New York, Washington, and Colorado, compensation may fall outside the listed range.

jobs.title: Tech Product Manager Platform
jobs.jd_full:
Esta posición es requerida para acompañar a los equipos de Plataformas de Passenger y Aircraft. Requerida experiencia previa como Tech Product Manager.
Conocimientos específicos para acompañamiento de equipos Platform-as-a-Product:
  • Comprensión del enfoque Platform-as-a-Product
  • Fluidez técnica conceptual para facilitar conversaciones sobre arquitectura, escalabilidad, APIs, datos, confiabilidad, seguridad, observabilidad, dependencias, deuda técnica y costo.
  • Habilidad para conectar iniciativas técnicas con impacto de negocio,
  • Experiencia guiando priorización de portfolio de plataforma
  • Conocimiento de métricas de plataforma

  • Experiencia en gestión de stakeholders cross-team

jobs.title: Strategic Project Manager
jobs.jd_full:
Strategic Project Manager

Full-time, Remote (aligned to EST business hours)

Check out The Global Talent Co.’s talent network:https://www.globaltalent.co/network

About Us

At The Global Talent Co., we provide opportunities to work with leading innovative technology companies worldwide, offering stable employment, competitive compensation, career growth, and access to a community of 25,000+ like-minded marketing professionals.

About the Company

You will be joining a fast-scaling D2C brand in the pet wellness space that’s redefining how people feed their pets. The company combines the agility of a startup with the resources and structure of a global organization. Founded to disrupt the traditional pet food industry, they offer healthy, vet-developed recipes made with wholesome ingredients that pet parents can trust. The brand is on a mission to give every pet a seat at the table — and to help them live happier, healthier lives.

The Role

As a Strategic Project Manager, you will play a key role in driving high-impact initiatives across strategy, analytics, and retail partnerships. Reporting to the Senior Strategy Manager, this role is ideal for a proactive, analytical, and entrepreneurial individual who thrives in a fast-paced, cross-functional environment. You’ll help manage strategic projects end-to-end — from ideation and analysis to execution and measurement — ensuring key business priorities move forward efficiently.

This position offers the opportunity to gain broad exposure to all areas of the business while helping shape the next phase of growth for a mission-driven brand.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead and manage strategic and cross-functional projects from conception through implementation

  • Support the development and execution of retention and customer growth initiatives

  • Conduct in-depth market, competitive, and customer analyses to identify opportunities and inform strategic decisions

  • Perform analytical deep dives into key business metrics, uncovering drivers of performance and actionable insights

  • Build and maintain complex business models in Google Sheets or Excel to forecast and evaluate initiatives

  • Manage regular business reporting and dashboards using Google Sheets, Excel, and Tableau

  • Support sales and retail partnership activities — including outreach, sales material creation, and campaign management

  • Collaborate closely with internal stakeholders across departments to ensure alignment and timely delivery of projects

  • Contribute to business case development, reporting, and process improvements

  • Proactively identify opportunities for efficiency, scalability, and growth within the organization

Ideal Candidate

  • 3–5 years of experience in strategy, project management, or management consulting, ideally within a fast-paced or high-growth environment

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with experience handling large and complex data sets

  • Advanced proficiency in Google Sheets or Excel; familiarity with Tableau is a plus (and teachable)

  • Proven ability to manage and execute complex cross-functional projects

  • Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively across teams and influence stakeholders

  • Highly organized, structured, and detail-oriented project manager

  • Entrepreneurial mindset — creative, resourceful, and motivated to drive measurable impact

  • Comfortable moving between strategic planning and hands-on execution

  • Passion for improving the lives of pets and interest in the pet wellness or consumer goods space

Join Our Team

Be part of a collaborative, fast-growing environment where your analytical and project management skills can make a real impact. We offer competitive compensation, professional development opportunities, and a supportive remote culture that encourages innovation, ownership, and growth. Apply now and help shape the future of pet wellness.

At The Global Talent Co., our mission is to bridge the gap between leading global tech companies and amazing global talent. We believe everyone deserves equal access to opportunities, regardless of the country they are born in.

Originally posted on Himalayas

jobs.title: Product Manager, SEO & Growth (Remote)
jobs.jd_full:
About Trellis:

Trellis (https://trellis.law/) is the largest legal research platform for state trial courts, with coverage spanning thousands of courts and millions of cases across the U.S. We aggregate and structure trial court filings, rulings, motions, and outcomes into a unified, searchable platform, combining large-scale data infrastructure with AI-powered analysis to extract, organize, and surface insights across that data. This makes large-scale trial court research possible for the first time.

Our tools enable attorneys to analyze judge tendencies, evaluate opposing counsel, find and assess expert witnesses, and see which arguments and strategies have succeeded in similar cases, so they can assess risk earlier, save time on research, and develop more effective, data-backed case strategies.

About the Role:

Trellis is looking for a Product Manager, Growth to own two key growth levers: bringing the right buyers in through organic search and converting them through a strong product-led experience.

This role will split time between product-led growth and SEO, owning the connection between acquisition, activation, trial conversion and expansion. You’ll report to our Director of Product and work closely with Engineering and Design to define priorities, write clear requirements, unblock execution and measure impact through Amplitude and/or GSC.

This is a great role for someone who is analytical, technical enough to partner closely with Engineering and comfortable turning ambiguous growth opportunities into clear, measurable product work.

What You’ll Do:

Own Product-Led Growth

  • Own the activation funnel from first session through paid conversion.
  • Instrument the PLG funnel in Amplitude and define key activation benchmarks.
  • Run structured experiments to reduce friction and improve time-to-first-value.
  • Partner with Sales and Customer Success on trial, conversion and expansion motions.
  • Connect PLG initiatives directly to activation and conversion metrics.

Own SEO & Organic Discovery

  • Identify coverage gaps across Trellis’s large programmatic SEO footprint.
  • Prioritize which case types, courts and court slugs should be expanded for indexing.
  • Own canonical URL strategy and MCP/UTM attribution governance at scale.
  • Partner with Engineering on schema, indexing infrastructure and court taxonomy expansion.
  • Track organic acquisition through in-product activation in Amplitude and GSC.

Drive Roadmap, Delivery, and Reporting

  • Maintain a clear, written roadmap for PLG and SEO initiatives.
  • Write scoped product requirements that Engineering can execute without unnecessary back-and-forth.
  • Run sprint priorities and unblock Engineering and Design independently.
  • Proactively surface blockers, risks, and trade-offs to stakeholders.
  • Deliver a consistent weekly written update covering roadmap status, experiment results, key decisions and blockers.

Who You Are:

  • 3–5 years of product management experience, ideally with hands-on PLG, growth, activation, or funnel ownership.
  • Hands-on SEO experience, especially technical SEO or programmatic SEO at a meaningful scale.
  • Proficiency with GSC and Amplitude, or a similar product analytics platform.
  • Strong comfort working with Engineering on technical requirements and implementation trade-offs.
  • Excellent async communication skills, including PRDs, roadmap updates, Slack communication, and stakeholder updates that do not require constant follow-up.

Nice to have:

  • Experience in legal tech, court data, B2B SaaS or another complex data environment.
  • Experience with AI-powered product features, MCP integrations, or attribution systems.
  • Familiarity with large-scale indexing, schema, metadata, taxonomy or URL governance.

Benefits:

  • Meaningful equity
  • Full Medical, dental, and vision coverage (for you and your family). ALL premiums covered!
  • 401k w/ full match up to state limit
  • Work from anywhere, but be willing to travel for sales meetings, leadership retreats, etc
  • Flexible vacation policy. You manage your own time

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

jobs.title: Product Manager
jobs.jd_full:
Who we are?

Silverfin is a cutting-edge cloud software solution built by accountants, for accountants.

Founded in 2013, Silverfin was created to solve the frustrations of manual, error-prone processes in analysing financial data and compliance. From day one, we’ve been a cloud-first platform with an open API architecture, leveraging structured data to transform how accountants work.

Our mission is simple: to enhance and support the accountancy profession through AI and automation, ensuring accountants remain relevant and respected in the future of finance.

In 2023, Silverfin became part of the Visma Group, joining one of Europe’s leading providers of cloud software. This partnership strengthens our ability to innovate and expand our impact within the accountancy industry.

Today, Silverfin is trusted by over 1000+ firms across 19 countries. Our clients include all of the Big 4 accounting firms, as well as leading global networks like BDO, PKF, Baker Tilly, Grant Thornton, and Moore.

Headquartered in Ghent, with offices in London, Luxembourg, Gouda (NL), and a 2nd office in South of Belgium (Mont-Saint-Guibert), we’re a team of over 200 people - including a diverse remote engineering team working across multiple time zones - all working together to drive innovation in accounting.

How would you support our success?

That mission is still standing strong, and we’ve made a bet on where the work is heading. An accountant won’t click through screens to fill in data and assemble an answer. They’ll expect it to be done already or to be able to just ask for it. “I’m meeting this client tomorrow, help me prepare.” “If the current numbers hold to year-end, how much should they prepay next quarter? Draft them the message.” “What’s the most this business can pay out in dividends? Prepare the distribution tests for me to discuss with the customer.”.

Everyone will have agents that attempt to get those answers. The scarce thing (and the bet) is whether the answer is one an accountant can put their name under. Our compliance engine is what makes it so. While agents play a big role in getting to compliance deliverables, there’s a deterministic, controlled and auditable core.

Turning that into insights into the hands of the accountant and their clients is your team’s mission.

What would you do to drive our success?

The territory: every touchpoint where an accounting firm’s work reaches its client: data visualisation, exports, the client conversations, the thread between meetings, and, increasingly, the agents on top of all of it. A lot of what worked well yesterday needs to be questioned. You own the experience of the end client on the other side of those moments: the person our customer serves.

What you’re walking into: there is a foundation to build on but there’s plenty of opportunities remaining to be discovered and delivered on. Our customers want us to keep focus on this, so you’ll need to iterate and keep the pace high, while keeping the outcome in the center of the decisions you make.

What would success look like?

  • Customer value at pace. This is the one thing you care about most, you want to keep a team focused and moving at pace. You have the spine to say no to protect the one or two things that matter from the dozen that don’t.
  • Discovery rigour. You’re in front of real users constantly to learn and validate what you’re doing (Teresa Torres’ continuous discovery habits are our foundation). You kill the ideas that don’t survive contact with users as readily as you ship the ones that do.
  • You inspire. People across the organisation understand what you’re doing and why. You set people up for success when telling customers about this cool thing Silverfin is doing, and you’re not too shy to go to customer events and be in front of an audience occasionally.

Who you are

The ground under this role will evolve quickly. The agentic shift means the territory you own today won’t be the territory next year. Some of what you build will be questioned, merged, or reinvented. If you need a stable lane to do your best work, this will frustrate you. If redrawing the map is a part you enjoy, you’ll thrive.

You don’t need to have been a PM before. If you’ve spent years across the table from business owners and found yourself redesigning the workflow instead of just running it: questioning why the work is done this way, prototyping your own fixes, dragging your firm toward better tools… that instinct is what we’re hiring. We’ll teach you the craft: you’ll work daily with a strong product designer, learn continuous discovery inside a team that already practices it, get coaching from product leadership, and have budget for training. What we can’t teach is the judgment that comes from sitting with real clients. That’s the part you bring.

You’re AI-native in your own work. You reach for AI by default: to draft, to analyse, to prototype; and because the capabilities keeps moving, you keep re-testing where it is. What it couldn’t do last quarter it might do now: you’re the person who notices quickly and changes their opinion.

How we work

  • We’re impatient, on purpose. We’d rather put a rough version in front of a user this week than a polished one next month. If your instinct is to de-risk everything before showing it, we’ll clash; if your instinct is to learn in public, we’ll get along.
  • AI is a daily tool. We prototype with it, test with it, and expect you to. You don’t need to do everything yourself, but “I’ll wait for the team to prototype this” is the wrong reflex here.
  • Product carries real weight in the trio. Our engineers and designers are strong partners, but they look to product to drive direction and make the call. If you want alignment handed to you, this isn’t the place.
  • Strategy grows from the ground up. Direction isn’t a finished vision cascading down, teams closest to users keep shaping a large share of it. That’s the best thing about this job if you want to do strategy, and the worst thing if you want to execute someone else’s.

We care for you

  • Be part of something bigger – Join a growing SaaS company that’s now proudly part of Visma, Europe’s leader in mission-critical cloud software.
  • A connected team– Take part in regular team activities, retreats, and offsites to keep our culture thriving.
  • Grow with us – Take advantage of career development opportunities and a personal training allowance to support your growth.
  • Time to recharge – Enjoy our company-wide Wellbeing Days off each month (reviewed annually).
  • Fair compensation– Receive a competitive rewards package with benefits tailored to your role.
  • Flexibility with connection – This is primarily a hybrid role based in our Ghent HQ, with a minimum of two days a week in the office. For the right candidate, we’re open to exploring a hybrid setup based from our London office instead. We understand that life can be unpredictable, so we also offer flexibility to accommodate unexpected events.
  • Work where you thrive – Enjoy the freedom of a fully remote setup while staying connected through virtual collaboration

How we work

At Silverfin, we’re an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, or disability status. We’re committed to elevating talent by creating an environment where we can all thrive together.

We have a unique culture with our values ​​as the backbone:

Who we are - Authentically human

How we think - With a growth mindset

How we act- Bravely,  side by side

How we win - By doing what we love

At Silverfin we make our own way. We are constantly looking for new and innovative ways to move forward. Continuous learning and development is encouraged to support your personal and professional growth.

At the same time, this opens up many new opportunities that allow our people to grow with Silverfin, and often faster than with other companies. So many ambitious team members have already found their way to a new challenge within Silverfin.

We want you to reach your full potential. To get you there, we don’t want to think in boxes and over-structure things. So your options are not limited to 1 team or 1 career track. Your talent, motivation and potential play a crucial role in evolving across the teams into new roles.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

jobs.title: Product Manager IV
jobs.jd_full:
Join Aya Healthcare, winner of multiple Top Workplace awards!

This forward thinking Product Manager will manage an external facing software product with AI/ML components. They will work with software engineering and the product team to improve our products, address new challenges, and drive business outcomes for Aya. They need to be able to break down a complex and technical roadmap into software requirements that iteratively deliver value, and also be able to effectively communicate a business-focused vision to our stakeholders and internal leadership. Previous experience working with products that contain AI/ML components and/or related to healthcare staffing is highly desired.

Who We Are:

We’re an $8+ billion, rapidly growing workforce solutions provider in the healthcare industry. We deliver tech-enabled services that help healthcare organizations meet and manage their contingent labor needs. We build and manage tech-enabled marketplaces for national and local healthcare talent and deliver contingent labor management solutions through our proprietary software platform.

At Aya, we’re obsessed with creating exceptional experiences for our clients, clinicians, and employees. In fact, we put employee satisfaction above all else. Our team members are responsible for incomparable customer experience and we know that happy employees are critical to maintaining happy clients. We foster an entrepreneurial, high-energy, low-bureaucracy culture and value innovative thinking and creative problem solving. We embrace diversity in thought and backgrounds unified by a commitment to high achievement. When you join Aya, you’ll be surrounded by teammates who care about you as an individual and leaders who will help you grow both personally and professionally.

Responsibilities:

  • On-going development of the product vision, strategy, and roadmap
  • Drive discovery activities – working with users, design and, technical team members – to create effective solutions that will deliver quantifiable value to Aya and our clients.
  • Lead cross-functional teams to collaboratively realize product concepts and bring them to market.
  • Produce quality work while maintaining a strong sense of urgency
  • Respond to changing market and business dynamics quickly, with effective collaboration with stakeholders, the delivery team and leadership
  • Support Production Support team with analyzing escalated issues

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience.
  • 10+ years of experience working on an external facing software product while in a Business Analyst, Product Manager, or Product Owner role
  • Experience with healthcare staffing, workforce planning, scheduling software, processes or integrations is preferred
  • AI/ML experience a plus
  • Demonstrated understanding of the techniques and methods of modern product discovery and product delivery.
  • Proven ability to engage with engineers, designers, and company leaders in a constructive and collaborative relationship.
  • Excellent communication and presentation skills (verbal and written), initiative and responsiveness.
  • Excellent interpersonal skills with proven ability to influence with impact across functions and disciplines.

What We Offer:

  • Free premium medical, dental, life and vision insurance
  • Generous 401(k) match
  • Aya also offers other benefits to those that are eligible and where required by applicable law, including reimbursements and discretionary bonuses
  • Aya provides paid sick leave in accordance with all applicable state, federal, and local laws. Aya’s general sick leave policy is that employees accrue one hour of paid sick leave for every 30 hours worked. However, to the extent any provisions of the statement above conflict with any applicable paid sick leave laws, the applicable paid sick leave laws are controlling
  • Celebrations! We hit our goals and reward ourselves.
  • Company-sponsored virtual events, happy hours and team-building activities are always on the horizon — plus, you get a special treat on your birthday!
  • Unlimited DTO — we believe in time off!
  • Virtual yoga, meditation or boot camp classes offered daily

Compensation: Aya reasonably anticipates the pay scale for this position to be an annual salary of $185,000 to $205,000.

The pay scale for this position may vary if applicant possesses experience outside of what Aya reasonably anticipates for this position. Bonuses are subject to the role and your manager’s discretion.

Aya is an Equal Opportunity Employer (EEO), including Disability / Vets, and welcomes all to apply. Please click here for our EEO policy.

jobs.title: Payments Product Manager
jobs.jd_full:
We’re currently looking for a Payments Product Manager who will take a key role on our United States location-neutral team. You’ll serve as CardFlight’s payments domain expert. Bringing leadership, expertise, and experience across transaction networks and methods, processor integrations and certifications, compliance mandates, card brand relationships, and emerging payment standards, while serving as the connective tissue among the payments team, engineering, and the broader product organization.

You’ll interpret product strategy, technical specifications and translate complex requirements into engineering-ready stories. This is a product management role at its core — you will own the strategy, requirements, and roadmap for our payments infrastructure. The nature of payments integrations and certifications means a meaningful portion of your time also involves project-level execution, but the job is defined by product thinking, not task coordination.

In this role, we value someone who brings deep payments expertise and a genuine customer-centric product mindset, combined with deep understanding of the context and constraints in payment transaction processing, and a pragmatism and solution-oriented mindset to create and ship elegant solutions despite lots of complexity below the surface.  You thrive in technical ambiguity, operate with a high degree of self-sufficiency, and understand that in payments, the details matter as much as the strategy.

What You’ll Do

Payments Strategy & Certification Ownership

  • Providing product thought leadership on how all transactional components of our product function, how that maps to processor integrations, our roadmap for end-to-end certification processes, while working alongside the certification team on execution.
  • Managing processor integration and certification processes end-to-end, from intake paperwork through testing and delivery, including negotiating scope and requirements with certification analysts at major processors.
  • Serving as the primary point of contact with processors and card brands for compliance matters, emerging standards, and production issues, with the communication skills and relationship-building discipline to represent CardFlight effectively in high-stakes technical conversations.
  • Reviewing card brand compliance bulletins and emerging payments standards, developing a pragmatic point of view on where the product needs to evolve, surfacing and actioning those recommendations proactively.
  • Acting as CardFlight’s internal expert on topics like emerging payment methods and standards, EMV kernels, certification intake requirements, and processor integration architecture, helping the broader team build knowledge.
  • Managing many concurrent workstreams across multiple active certifications and processor integrations simultaneously without losing attention to detail.

Technical Requirements & Spec Interpretation

  • Reading and interpreting raw specification documents from card brands and processors, and translating them into clear, engineering-ready requirements, user stories, and Product Requirements Documents without requiring them to be pre-interpreted by someone else.
  • Identifying compliance obligations from standards bodies like EMVCo and PCI-DSS, card brand rules and processor integration specs, and proactively surfacing requirements before they become production issues.
  • Assessing how new user-facing features impact processor integrations and certification requirements, and flagging those dependencies early in the product development process.
  • Advocating for payments capabilities such as emerging authentication standards, network tokenization, or new payment methods by building the product case before they become urgent priorities.

Roadmap Stewardship & Launch Readiness

  • Maintaining and executing against a roadmap that balances active certifications, new processor integrations, compliance obligations, and platform improvements.
  • Acting as the bridge between the payments team and the broader product organization, ensuring payments infrastructure priorities are visible, understood, and accounted for in the overall product roadmap.
  • Clearly communicating the reasoning behind roadmap decisions to engineering, operations, and partner teams so everyone understands not just what is being built but why.
  • Owning launch readiness by coordinating across teams to ensure documentation, certification sign-off, and engineering work are all aligned and complete before anything ships.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

  • Deeply embedded in the payments team’s culture and daily work, serving as a trusted partner to the certification team while bringing a product management lens to their priorities and processes.
  • Embedded in the engineering team’s daily rhythm, attending standups, refinements, and sprint planning, and reviewing completed work to ensure it meets the intended product outcome before release.
  • Working with operations to share product requirements, review documentation, and train the team on how new features or compliance changes work so they can effectively support merchants after launch.

What You’ll Bring to the Table

  • At least 3 years (and preferably 5-10+ years) of product management experience in payments. What matters is that you have built deep expertise in payments infrastructure, have overseen integrations into payment processors and experience with their certification processes, and that you are familiar with shipping software that is developed and maintained in-house.
  • Comfort with the project-management overhead that payments certifications require tracking timelines, managing external partners, and coordinating across teams while keeping the product perspective front and center.
  • Deep understanding of processor certification processes (including “Level 3” card present certifications) and hands-on experience managing certifications with major processors and card brands. If this doesn’t resonate, this role is likely not the right fit.
  • Familiarity with applicable standards like PCI DSS and EMVCo kernel lifecycles.
  • Ability to read, interpret, and translate raw processor integration specs and card brand rules into clear engineering requirements without needing them pre-interpreted by someone else.
  • Proven experience managing relationships with processors and card brands, with the communication skills and professionalism to represent a smaller organization effectively in high-stakes technical conversations.
  • The ability to prioritize effectively across a complex, ever-shifting set of payments obligations, certifications, compliance mandates, processor integrations, and product requests, and communicate those priorities clearly to the teams depending on you.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the organizational discipline to manage multiple deadlines and work with external partners simultaneously.
  • A natural collaborator who can bring together engineering, operations, and external partners to move complex technical work forward.
  • Comfort working with product analytics tools and the ability to use data to support and defend product decisions.

Who We Are

We are a leading payments technology company that empowers small businesses across the United States to accept payments seamlessly through our flagship product, SwipeSimple. We distribute our solutions primarily through a robust network of hundreds of merchant acquirers and independent sales organizations (ISOs), who in turn onboard thousands of merchants onto the SwipeSimple platform each month.

We are backed by private equity, including WestView Capital Partners, and are at an exciting inflection point in our growth journey.  We have a strong foundation of profitable recurring revenue today, and are focused on scaling over the next three to four years through optimizing our core channel, accelerating our direct-to-merchant offering, expanding value-added services, and selectively entering new markets.

Why You’ll Love Being Part of the CardFlight Team

  • US location-neutral work environment

  • Home-office equipment stipend

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Employee Engagement (Lunch & Learns, team building events)

  • Learning & Development culture

  • Comprehensive Health Benefits

  • Competitive compensation including:

    • Annualized Base Salary Range: $120,000 – $180,000
    • Annualized Company Performance Bonus: $8,000 – $20,000
    • Company Stock Options: 10,000

  • Remote flexibility and autonomy

jobs.title: Product Manager (contract)
jobs.jd_full:
Mindgrub Technologies is an award-winning agency whose mission is to drive efficiency, growth, and ROI through AI and digital innovation. We leverage a deep understanding of technology and growth strategy to deliver multi-channel solutions for enterprise clients, with a strong focus on the energy and utilities sectors.

Position Overview

Mindgrub is seeking a Product Manager to join our Product Strategy team. This highly collaborative role will partner with senior product leaders, designers, engineers, project managers, quality assurance, and client stakeholders to define, build, and deliver custom digital products for enterprise organizations.

This position will initially support multiple strategic client engagements, working alongside a Senior Product Manager to facilitate discovery, manage product backlogs, define requirements, and guide software delivery from concept through launch.

The ideal candidate enjoys solving complex business problems, thrives in collaborative environments, and is comfortable bringing structure to ambiguous situations. They possess an owner’s mindset, communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders, and are eager to learn new industries and business domains.

Mindgrub embraces modern AI-assisted ways of working. Candidates should be comfortable incorporating generative AI tools into research, analysis, documentation, backlog management, and day-to-day product delivery while applying sound judgment and maintaining high standards for quality and accuracy.

This position requires strong communication skills, exceptional organization, intellectual curiosity, and a passion for building software that creates meaningful business value. If you thrive at the intersection of strategy, collaboration, and execution, we’d love to hear from you.

Responsibilities

  • Represent the voice of the customer, advocating for user and business needs throughout product delivery.
  • Collaborate with senior product leaders to define product strategy, priorities, and delivery plans.
  • Facilitate workshops focused on discovery, requirements gathering, process mapping, ideation, prioritization, and solution definition.
  • Gather, analyze, and document business requirements from client stakeholders.
  • Create, refine, and maintain product backlogs, user stories, acceptance criteria, and release priorities.
  • Participate fully in Agile ceremonies including backlog refinement, sprint planning, reviews, retrospectives, and other product delivery activities.
  • Coordinate closely with UX designers, software engineers, QA, project managers, and client stakeholders throughout delivery.
  • Build trusted relationships with clients through thoughtful communication, collaboration, and problem solving.
  • Identify risks, assumptions, dependencies, and opportunities early, helping teams make informed decisions.
  • Contribute to product roadmaps and release planning alongside senior product leadership.
  • Partner with Mindgrub Account Managers and Project Managers to ensure successful client engagements and software delivery.
  • Leverage generative AI tools to improve research, documentation, backlog refinement, meeting preparation, analysis, and other product management activities while validating outputs for quality and accuracy.
  • Continuously improve product management processes, team collaboration, and delivery practices.

Requirements

  • Must be a U.S. Citizen
  • Remote; 9:00am - 5:00pm EST; 30 - 40 hrs / wk
  • July through February with potential to extend.
  • 2 - 6 years of experience managing digital products or custom software delivery in an Agile environment.
  • Experience partnering with cross-functional software development teams, including design, engineering, quality assurance, and project management.
  • Demonstrated ability to gather requirements, facilitate stakeholder discussions, and translate business needs into actionable product backlogs.
  • Experience creating and maintaining user stories, acceptance criteria, and product backlogs throughout the software development lifecycle.
  • Working knowledge of Agile delivery methodologies, including Scrum, Kanban, or hybrid approaches.
  • Excellent written, verbal, presentation, and interpersonal communication skills.
  • Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities with a demonstrated ability to navigate ambiguity.
  • Experience building productive relationships with enterprise clients or internal business stakeholders.
  • Experience using generative AI tools (such as ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Microsoft Copilot, or equivalents) to improve productivity, research, documentation, backlog refinement, and other product management workflows while validating outputs for quality and accuracy.
  • Curiosity, adaptability, and the ability to quickly learn new industries, business domains, and technologies.
  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent professional experience. Five additional years of relevant on-the-job experience may substitute for a degree.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience delivering enterprise SaaS products in addition to experience delivering custom software solutions.
  • Experience working within consulting, digital agency, or professional services environments.
  • Experience supporting enterprise clients in complex industries such as legal services, aerospace, healthcare, government, financial services, manufacturing, or energy.
  • Familiarity with Jira, Confluence, Miro, Figma, Azure DevOps, or similar collaboration and product management tools.
  • Experience delivering AI-enabled products.
  • Experience facilitating client workshops, design thinking sessions, or product discovery engagements.

Compensation

  • $75.00 - $85.00 USD per hour
  • Base compensation will be determined based on a variety of factors including skills, education, certifications, geographic location, and overall experience relevant to the role.

Inclusivity without exception | At Mindgrub we value and encourage mutual respect and acceptance. We know we all have something to contribute. We work every day to ensure all are welcome, and that we treat all people with fairness and dignity.

No phone calls or recruiters, please.

#LI-Remote

jobs.title: Sr Technical Project Manager (Citizen/PR only)
jobs.jd_full:
Sr Technical Project Manager, Professional Services

(Singapore)

Created in 2000, Ivalua is a leading global provider of cloud-based procurement solutions.

COMPANY OVERVIEW

At Ivalua we are a global community of exceptional professionals, who believe that digital transformation revolutionises supply chain sustainability and resiliency to unlock the power of supplier collaboration.

We achieve this through our leading cloud-based spend management platform that empowers hundreds of the world’s most admired brands to effectively manage all categories of spend and all suppliers to increase profitability, improve ESG (environmental, social, and corporate governance) performance, lower risk, and improve productivity. Driven by our passions and fueled by our shared ambitions, we empower and challenge each other to create meaningful experiences for our colleagues, customers, partners, and communities.

Learn more at www.ivalua.com. Follow us on LinkedIn.

THE OPPORTUNITY

CONTEXT:

You will be part of the Professional Services Team whose mission is to successfully deliver our unique and configurable solution to customers and support our Partner community with high quality standards and a strong commitment to excellence and efficiency. Our team is very hands-on with a strong mindset of problem-solving while also having the ability to think holistically and provide solutions that solve the customer’s long-term challenges.

ROLE:

This is an exciting opportunity for a Project Manager at Ivalua. As a “Magic Quadrant” leader, our solutions work in a complex global economy, automating customized workflows to request, procure, receive, and pay for goods and services. Our innovative solutions refine the procurement lifecycle while reducing cost and risk.

We are seeking a highly experienced and detail-oriented Project Manager to lead the successful delivery of multiple, large-scale implementation projects. The ideal candidate is a strong PMO professional with a proven track record of managing complex programs, enforcing governance, and driving process excellence across a portfolio of projects. You will be responsible for ensuring project consistency, mitigating risks, and delivering on time and on budget.

WHAT YOU WILL DO WITH US

  • Lead and manage the end-to-end delivery of large-scale enterprise software implementations, ensuring alignment with program goals and business objectives.
  • Develop and enforce robust PMO standards, methodologies, and best practices across all projects under the program.
  • Serve as the primary point of contact for executive-level customer stakeholders and manage relationships with systems integrators and partners.
  • Proactively identify and manage program-level risks, issues, and dependencies.
  • Provide strategic guidance and oversight to individual project teams (Project Managers, Implementation Consultants, and Integration Consultants).
  • Monitor program budget, resources, scope and timelines to ensure projects are delivered on time and within scope.
  • Provide expertise on best practices, change management, and strategies to drive product adoption.
  • Drive continuous improvement by analyzing project performance metrics and implementing corrective actions.
  • Ensure a smooth transition from project go-live to post-production support.
  • Manage customer expectations and interact efficiently with Customer executives

YOUR PROFILE

If you have the below experience and strengths this role could be for you:

  • Minimum 8 years of experience in managing complex, enterprise-level software implementation projects or programs.
  • Ability to manage complex projects with global delivery centers/teams
  • Demonstrated experience in a leadership role with a strong PMO background.
  • Expertise in formal project management methodologies (e.g., PMP or equivalent) is a must.
  • Broad knowledge of enterprise-level hardware and software products, operating systems, and methodologies for integrating web-based products.
  • Exceptional leadership, communication, and negotiation skills, with the ability to influence C-level stakeholders.
  • Deliver compelling presentations that effectively convey information to a variety of audiences, including C-level executives.
  • Strong analytical skills with a focus on data-driven decision-making.
  • A Bachelor’s (BS) degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a closely related field or foreign equivalent is preferred.

Soft Skills :

  • Expertise in formal project management methodologies (e.g., PMP, PRINCE2, or equivalent) is a plus.
  • A Bachelor’s (BS) degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Business, or a closely related field or foreign equivalent is preferred.
  • Some travel may be necessary (~20%)
  • You will go through Ivalua’s certification process to learn the Product.

WHAT HAPPENS NEXT

If your application fits this specific position’s needs, our skilled Talent team will reach out to schedule an initial screening call. Get one step closer to achieving your goals – apply today!

Our Talent team will guide you through every step of the interview process - from preparation to completion. They’re here to support you!

Our recruitment process is designed to assess your competencies through a series of personalised interviews with internal stakeholders relevant to the role.

Interviews will be conducted virtually via video or on-site with face-to-face meetings.

LIFE AT IVALUA

  • Hybrid working model (3 days in the office per week),
  • We’re a team dedicated to pushing the boundaries of product innovation and technology,
  • Sustainable Growth, Privately Held,
  • A stable and cash-flow positive Company since 10 years,
  • Snacks and weekly lunches in the office,
  • Feel empowered to pursue your goals with improved team collaboration and increased creativity/productivity,
  • Unlock and unleash your full professional potential with our exceptional training and career development program,
  • Join a dynamic and international team of top-notch professionals who are experts in their respective fields. Collaborate with like-minded individuals who are deeply passionate and highly motivated about their work. Experience a truly diverse and inclusive work environment where your unique contributions are highly valued,
  • Regular social events, competitive outings, team running events, and musical activities,
  • Comparably recognized Ivalua for the following (https://www.comparably.com/companies/ivalua) :

Powered by People - Powered by You!

United by our values we embrace diversity and equity in the broadest possible sense to create an inclusive workplace. To help our customers make supply chains more efficient, sustainable and resilient, we rely on a global team with a variety of backgrounds, skills and views. We believe in equal opportunity and in diversity as a driver of innovation that cultivates a spirit of inclusiveness, creates a productive and fun place to work, and provides fulfilling career opportunities for all Ivaluans.

Experience life at Ivalua - check out our captivating video ! Gain insight into our unique company culture and get a glimpse of what it’s like to work with us.

#LI-AY1

#LI-HYBRID

jobs.title: Senior Program Manager, Commercial Strategy
jobs.jd_full:
Squarespace is looking for a Senior Program Manager to support the Commercial Strategy team’s mission to accelerate revenue growth by ensuring the team performs at its best with efficiency. Your responsibilities will include ensuring clear project/program planning, transparent progress tracking, risk mitigation, team process improvements, and efficient and scalable delivery. You will help connect the dots across people and projects, and proactively surface opportunities to go beyond the status quo.

The Commercial Strategy team accelerates revenue growth data-driven strategies that optimize pricing, product monetization, and profitability. We drive alignment across the company to ensure successful execution of various initiatives, partnering with product, business, and engineering teams. You will report to the Director of Commercial Strategy and work hybrid from our NYC office 2-3 days per week.

You’ll Get To…

Project/Program Management

  • Build and maintain clear project plans covering scope, milestones, owners, risks, dependencies, and issue tracking.
  • Break down silos, surface conflicting priorities, and ensure coordinated execution, including simultaneous launches across multiple product areas.
  • Track and communicate project status to stakeholders; own recurring executive-level communications and leadership agenda updates. Use AI tools to synthesize meeting outputs and draft communications at pace.
  • Facilitate cross-functional collaboration across Product, Engineering, Analytics, UXR, Tax, Legal, and Finance.
  • Manage A/B test pipeline sequencing across concurrent experiments, tracking interdependencies and flagging downstream schedule impacts.

Team Operations & Processes

  • Design and implement sustainable processes and playbooks for collaborative, transparent execution, incorporating AI-assisted workflows where they improve speed or quality.
  • Identify bottlenecks and inefficiencies in team workflows and drive solutions, including evaluating and piloting AI tools that could meaningfully reduce manual effort.
  • Manage the team’s operating rhythm: project lifecycles, planning cycles, goal-setting, team rituals, and leadership updates.
  • Hold teams accountable for commitments and remove blockers.
  • Establish regular reviews of key pricing levers including foreign currencies, discounts, and optimization opportunities.
  • Create and maintain a source of truth for pricing and packaging decisions; drive knowledge-sharing best practices, including AI-assisted documentation to make institutional knowledge more accessible.

Who We’re Looking For

  • 5+ years of program or project management experience, driving cross-functional initiatives with stakeholders from diverse disciplines; preference for experience in a SaaS or software environment.
  • Strong knowledge of the product development lifecycle; familiarity with Agile, Scrum, and Waterfall methodologies.
  • Experience with A/B testing methodologies; working knowledge of statistical and financial analysis and qualitative/quantitative research.
  • Track record of bringing structure and predictability to a fast-paced environment with ease in context-switching.
  • Ability to influence without authority across functions and levels; exceptional communication and stakeholder management skills.
  • Proficiency in Jira, Google Workspace, Excel, Looker, Amplitude, and Statsig (or equivalent A/B testing platforms). Comfortable using AI tools (e.g. Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini) regularly to research, draft, and synthesize; demonstrated ability to evaluate and apply them critically.
  • Interest in software products, business strategy, pricing models, and competitive landscape.
  • Experience shipping products globally with an understanding of localization and internationalization is a plus.
  • Direct experience with online checkout, billing, payment processors, or pricing strategy is preferred.

Benefits & Perks

  • A choice between medical plans with an option for 100% covered premiums
  • Fertility and adoption benefits
  • Access to supplemental insurance plans for additional coverage
  • Headspace mindfulness app subscription
  • Global Employee Assistance Program
  • Retirement benefits with employer match
  • Flexible paid time off
  • 12 weeks paid parental leave and family care leave
  • Pretax commuter benefit
  • Education reimbursement
  • Employee donation match to community organizations
  • 7 Global Employee Resource Groups (ERGs)
  • Dog-friendly workplace
  • Free lunch and snacks
  • Private rooftop
  • Hack week twice per year

Cash Compensation Range: $126,500 - $203,550 USD

The base salary for this position will vary based on job-related criteria including relevant skills, experience, and location, among other factors.

In addition to the cash compensation above (which includes base salary and, where applicable for eligible roles, may include overtime pay), Squarespace employees are eligible to be granted an option to purchase our common stock. Sales positions generally offer a competitive On Target Earnings (OTE) incentive structure in addition to base salary.

About Squarespace

Squarespace is a design-driven platform helping entrepreneurs build brands and businesses online. We empower millions of customers in more than 200 countries and territories with all the tools they need to create an online presence, build an audience, monetize, and scale their business. Our suite of products range from websites, domains, ecommerce, and marketing tools, as well as tools for scheduling with Acuity and creating and managing social media presence with Bio Sites and Unfold. Our team of more than 1,700 is headquartered in New York City, with offices in Dublin, Ireland, and Aveiro, Portugal. For more information about our company, visit https://www.squarespace.com/about/careers.

Our Commitment

Today, more than a million people around the globe use Squarespace to share different perspectives and experiences with the world. Not only do we embrace and celebrate the diversity of our customer base, but we also strive for the same in our employees. At Squarespace, we are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, religion, national origin, gender, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital or parental status, disability, veteran status, or other class protected by applicable law. We are proud to be an equal opportunity workplace.

#LI-SN1 #LI-Hybrid

Thank you in advance for providing the following details about your work history from your resume! This helps us ensure that your candidate information is accurate and consistent during the hiring process.

Squarespace will never solicit your personal banking information or ask you to transfer money in connection with a job offer or interview. We also will not reach out to you via phone or SMS without your permission or knowledge.

If you plan to use AI in any capacity during your candidate journey, please review our Candidate AI Policy.

jobs.title: Delivery Manager (Google Workspace Services)
jobs.jd_full:
Overview of 66degrees

66degrees is an end-to-end AI transformation partner that guides enterprises from complex business challenges to clear, quantifiable outcomes. Our company is the culmination of several successful firms, each a leader in its own right in cloud, artificial intelligence, and data. This convergence of talent and expertise is how we help businesses reach their own “inflection point,” where chaotic data becomes a strategic asset, complexity becomes clarity, and AI becomes an engine for growth. Our ultimate vision is to be the catalyst for a future where every business operates as an intelligent enterprise, with autonomous systems unlocking human potential.

At 66degrees, we believe in thriving through challenges and winning together. These values not only guide us in achieving our goals as a company but also for our people. We are dedicated to creating a significant impact for our employees by fostering a culture that sparks innovation and supports professional and personal growth along the way.

Overview of Role

66degrees is looking for creative, dedicated, and personable technology business professionals for a Delivery Manager position. As a key contributor to the Technical Delivery team, you will help orchestrate the migration, security, and optimization of Google Workspace clients. As well as continue to assist the business as we develop more Google Cloud professional services offerings and delivery — putting a relentless focus on business outcomes at every step. Our ideal candidate will understand the value of teamwork. We are looking for the right fit with our growing organization and we will invest in training for the right individual.

Responsibilities:

  • Manage multiple concurrent projects from initial communication to project completion
  • Gather requirements from clients and communicate with technical team
  • Manage project scope, finances, schedule and expectations of all parties
  • Confirm resource availability and appointment
  • Coordinate internal and external resources for flawless execution of projects
  • Set client expectations and safeguard technical feasibility
  • Develop project plans, track progress and update appropriate parties
  • Hold weekly project updates covering financial and functional updates
  • Anything else that needs to be done that adds value to the business, our clients and your co-workers

Qualifications:

  • Relevant education or experience, preferred fields are computer science, engineering, or technical project management
  • Experience in infrastructure and/or application software migration project management (Preferably around collaboration tools O365, Google Workspace, etc)
  • Solid understanding of the software development lifecycle and web development
  • Excellent communication skills, written and verbal
  • Comfortable in presentation settings with C-level executives
  • Proven ability to manage multiple projects at a time while paying close attention to detail
  • Impeccable organizational and follow-up skills
  • Creative and strong problem-solving skills
  • Demonstrates team working abilities and high moral compass
  • Agile project management knowledge and application
  • Ability and desire to take initiative
  • Thrive in a fast-paced work environment
  • Ability to stay calm and defuse high stake situations
  • Ability to travel to client sites as needed and requested

Preferred Qualifications:

  • PMP/Scrum/Agile Certification and mastery
  • Previous Experience in depth experience with Google Workspace technically or in a project management capacity.

66degrees is an Equal Opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to actual or perceived race, color, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, national origin, age, weight, height, marital status, sexual orientation, veteran status, disability status or other legally protected class.

AI Transparency & Disclosure

As an AI transformation partner, 66degrees leverages intelligent solutions to enhance our recruitment experience. We utilize AI tools—including LinkedIn Recruiter’s Hiring Assistant and interview transcription technologies—to assist with sourcing, role analysis, and capturing interview highlights.

These tools augment our process, but we “Commit to Our Craft” by ensuring all final hiring decisions are made by our human Talent Team. By applying, you acknowledge the use of these technologies to help us “Win Together” in finding the best fit for our team.

jobs.title: Associate Delivery Manager
jobs.jd_full:
Overview of 66degrees

66degrees is an end-to-end AI transformation partner that guides enterprises from complex business challenges to clear, quantifiable outcomes. Our company is the culmination of several successful firms, each a leader in its own right in cloud, artificial intelligence, and data. This convergence of talent and expertise is how we help businesses reach their own “inflection point,” where chaotic data becomes a strategic asset, complexity becomes clarity, and AI becomes an engine for growth. Our ultimate vision is to be the catalyst for a future where every business operates as an intelligent enterprise, with autonomous systems unlocking human potential.

At 66degrees, we believe in thriving through challenges and winning together. These values not only guide us in achieving our goals as a company but also for our people. We are dedicated to creating a significant impact for our employees by fostering a culture that sparks innovation and supports professional and personal growth along the way.

Overview of Role

As an Associate Delivery Manager at 66degrees, you will play a vital role in supporting and managing the project life cycle for our client engagements, with a strong emphasis on leveraging agile delivery methodologies. This is a growth-oriented role designed for a professional looking to expand their delivery management career in a fast-paced, collaborative cloud consulting environment. You will work closely with senior delivery leaders and technical teams to ensure innovative solutions are executed effectively and delivered successfully to our clients.

Responsibilities:

  • Manage small-to-medium scale projects or specific workstreams within larger enterprise programs from initiation to completion, leveraging core agile principles.
  • Facilitate day-to-day operations for cross-functional teams (cloud, data, AI, application development) to maintain momentum and keep projects on track.
  • Lead daily standups, scrum ceremonies, and project status meetings, championing agile practices within the team.
  • Act as a key liason between customers and technical teams to document requirements, track project milestones, and log risks/issues to keep project scope clear.
  • Assist in resolving project issues and client concerns promptly, escalating to senior management when necessary to ensure uninterrupted progress.
  • Maintain project health by tracking scope, schedules, and deliverables. Ensure project management tools (e.g., JIRA, Asana, FinancialForce) are meticulously updated and RAAIDD maintained
  • Own and manage project financials and margin performance ‘as sold or modified with approval,’ tracking project burn and profitability alongside core schedules.
  • Prepare regular status updates, project retrospectives, and health reports for internal stakeholders, leadership, and client points of contact.
  • Actively develop your domain and technical knowledge regarding cloud and data solutions to better facilitate project discussions and decisions as well as maintaining required certifications.

Qualifications:

  • 2–4 years of relevant experience in project coordination, scrum mastering, or technology implementation support, ideally within a professional services or consulting environment.
  • Strong foundational understanding of Agile/Scrum methodologies. A Scrum/Agile certification (e.g., CSM, PSM) is highly preferred.
  • Hands-on experience with agile project management and collaboration tools such as JIRA, Confluence, Google Workspace, or Asana.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to clearly translate client needs into actionable team tasks.
  • Solution-focused mindset with the ability to stay organized and detailed-oriented while managing multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment.
  • A keen interest or basic background in software development and cloud technologies (familiarity with Google Cloud Platform is a major plus).
  • Capacity to travel to client sites as needed (<25%).
  • A Bachelor’s degree in Business, Management, Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.

66degrees is an Equal Opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to actual or perceived race, color, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, national origin, age, weight, height, marital status, sexual orientation, veteran status, disability status or other legally protected class.

AI Transparency & Disclosure

As an AI transformation partner, 66degrees leverages intelligent solutions to enhance our recruitment experience. We utilize AI tools—including LinkedIn Recruiter’s Hiring Assistant and interview transcription technologies—to assist with sourcing, role analysis, and capturing interview highlights.

These tools augment our process, but we “Commit to Our Craft” by ensuring all final hiring decisions are made by our human Talent Team. By applying, you acknowledge the use of these technologies to help us “Win Together” in finding the best fit for our team.
